name: PM-AggregateResourceData
description: Scheduled daily flow (or manual trigger). Aggregates resource assignments across all active projects into the ResourceCapacity SP list for cross-project utilization views.

trigger.note: Also add a manual trigger option (Instant) for on-demand refresh

actions.name: Delete_All_ResourceCapacity_Items
actions.action: Send an HTTP request to SharePoint
actions.note: Clear the ResourceCapacity list for full refresh. Use batch delete or loop.

then.action: Apply_to_each → Send an HTTP request to SharePoint

subactions.name: Get_Project_Tasks
subactions.action: Send an HTTP request to SharePoint

subactions.name: Extract_Unique_People
subactions.note: Extract distinct AssignedTo values from the task list. Handle Person column (may be object with DisplayName, Email) or text field.
subactions.expression: union(/* distinct AssignedTo values from all tasks */)

values.TotalDuration: sum of Duration across filtered tasks

flowBuildGuide.title: How to build this flow in Power Automate
steps: 1. Create a Scheduled cloud flow — Recurrence: every 1 day at 02:00 UTC
2. Also add a second trigger: 'Manually trigger a flow' (for on-demand refresh)
STEP 1 — Clear ResourceCapacity list:
a. SP HTTP GET: /_api/web/lists/getbytitle('ResourceCapacity')/items?$select=Id&$top=5000
b. Apply_to_each → SP HTTP POST with headers IF-MATCH:*, X-HTTP-Method:DELETE
URI: /_api/web/lists/getbytitle('ResourceCapacity')/items({Id})
STEP 2 — Get active projects:
SharePoint 'Get items' → List: ProjectRegistry, Filter: ProjectStatus eq 'Active'
STEP 3 — Loop each project:
Apply_to_each (concurrency=1 to avoid throttling):
3.1 SP HTTP GET tasks from the project's task list (use ProjectListName from the loop item)
3.2 Update ProjectRegistry.TaskCount = length of tasks array
3.3 Extract distinct people — this is the trickiest part:
- If AssignedTo is a Person column: it returns {DisplayName, Email, Id}
- If AssignedTo is a Text column: parse as-is
- Use Select action to extract unique names, then union() to deduplicate
3.4 For each person → Filter tasks → Compute aggregates → Create ResourceCapacity item
AGGREGATE FORMULAS (use in Compose actions):
TaskCount: @{length(body('Filter_Person_Tasks'))}
CompletedTasks: @{length(filter(body('Filter_Person_Tasks'), item()?['Status'] eq 'Completed'))}
TotalDuration: Use Select to extract Duration values, then sum (or loop + increment variable)
AvgPercentComplete: divide(sum of PercentComplete, TaskCount)
EarliestStart: min() — use Sort + first() pattern
LatestEnd: max() — use Sort(desc) + first() pattern
TIP: For sum/average in Power Automate without Dataverse:
Initialize variable varSum (Float, 0)
Apply_to_each task → Increment variable varSum by task.Duration
After loop: varSum / count = average
